review_notes: The functions are generally well-aligned with the NIST 800-53 AC-3(15) control, focusing on access enforcement through Kubernetes RBAC. The naming conventions mostly follow the prescribed pattern, but slight adjustments were made for consistency and clarity. The functions accurately reflect Kubernetes security controls related to RBAC.
naming_improvements: Use 'namespace_restricted' instead of 'to_specific_namespaces' for brevity and clarity
Use 'access_restricted' instead of 'restricted_access' to maintain consistent action-object order
title: Access Enforcement - Access enforcement | discretionary and mandatory access control (a) enforce [ as
step1_reasoning: The NIST 800-53 AC-3(15) control focuses on enforcing discretionary and mandatory access control, which aligns with Kubernetes RBAC mechanisms. Kubernetes RBAC allows for fine-grained access control through roles and bindings, similar to IAM roles and policies in cloud environments. The functions proposed ensure that no overly permissive roles like 'cluster-admin' are bound, enforce least privilege principles, restrict role bindings to specific namespaces, and ensure service accounts have restricted access, all of which contribute to discretionary and mandatory access control.
